behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: The core failure boundary is deterministic grouping logic: given parsed log records, grouping must key on trace ID and not service name. No OS/network/subprocess dependency is required, and mocking does not inherently hide this specific failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure grouping component with mixed-service input records sharing trace IDs (for example, auth/payment/api services). Assert one group per trace ID and that each group contains entries from all contributing services.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is primarily ordering/skew-correction logic over timestamps, which is deterministic and local. It does not require real infrastructure; a focused algorithm test can expose incorrect skew handling directly.
behaviors.test_description: Test the timeline ordering function with synthetic entries from multiple services where true causal order is known but timestamps differ by +/-500ms. Assert resulting timeline order matches expected corrected order; include boundary cases at exactly 500ms and just over 500ms.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: The behavior spans at least two modules: percentile/baseline computation and anomaly reporting output. A unit test of threshold math alone can miss real failures where computed spike status is not propagated or incorrectly rendered in the report.
behaviors.test_description: Run the local analysis pipeline over a fixture dataset with stable baseline windows and one injected high-latency window where p99 > 3x baseline. Assert anomaly detector marks spike and anomaly report includes the corresponding flag/entry.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: This is a module-interaction failure boundary: parser errors must be handled by pipeline control flow and reflected in reporting/metrics. A parser-only unit test would not prove the end-to-end skip-and-count behavior or crash resilience.
behaviors.test_description: Execute ingestion+analysis on a mixed fixture containing valid and malformed JSON lines. Assert process completes, valid entries are still analyzed, malformed lines are excluded, and reported skipped count equals malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real runtime characteristics (scheduling, buffering, backpressure, GC/CPU contention, I/O paths). Mocked or purely in-memory module tests can hide throughput/drop issues that only appear under real process/OS execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run a load test against the built tool process with a real burst generator at 10,000 entries/sec for a sustained interval. Measure ingest count vs. emitted/analyzed count, queue lag, and end-to-end delay; assert zero drops and lag remains within defined SLO.
